questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The bar chart shows four values: $10, 20, 30, 40$. Compute their total.

figure.caption: Bar chart of four values
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='30' y1='120' x2='210' y2='120' stroke='#475569'/><g fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'><rect x='45' y='100' width='25' height='20'/><rect x='85' y='80' width='25' height='40'/><rect x='125' y='60' width='25' height='60'/><rect x='165' y='40' width='25' height='80'/></g><text x='50' y='138' font-size='8'>10, 20, 30, 40</text></svg>

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The square root of $196$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Find the number whose square is $196$.

**Working.** $14^2 = 196$.

**Answer.** $14$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Choose the correct comparative form: 'This seam is ____ than the one we mined last year.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** One-syllable adjectives form the comparative with '-er', and 'than' signals a comparative (not superlative).

**Working.** 'Thick' becomes 'thicker'; 'more thicker' is a double comparative and 'thickest' is superlative.

**Answer.** thicker.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A is three times as old as B, and the sum of their ages is $48$ years. A's age is _____ years.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Express both ages in one variable.

**Working.** $A=3B$ and $A+B=48\Rightarrow 4B=48\Rightarrow B=12,\;A=36$.

**Answer.** $36$ years.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: An amount of $600$ is divided in the ratio $2:3$. The larger part is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each share equals the total times its ratio fraction.

**Working.** Larger fraction $=\dfrac{3}{2+3}=\dfrac{3}{5}$, so
$$600\times\frac{3}{5}=360$$

**Answer.** $360$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Choose the correct word: 'The blast had a serious ____ on the surrounding structures.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** 'Effect' is the noun (a result); 'affect' is usually the verb (to influence).

**Working.** A noun is needed after 'a serious', so 'effect' is correct.

**Answer.** effect.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Pointing to a man, a woman said, 'His mother is the only daughter of my mother.' How is the woman related to the man?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Resolve the description inside-out.

**Working.** 'The only daughter of my mother' is the woman herself, so 'his mother' is the woman — she is his mother.

**Answer.** Mother.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The square-dot pattern grows as $1, 4, 9, \ldots$ (perfect squares). The number of dots in the next figure is _____ .

figure.caption: Square dot arrangements of 1, 4 and 9 dots.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 90' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><circle cx='25' cy='45' r='4' fill='#6366f1'/><g fill='#6366f1'><circle cx='80' cy='35' r='4'/><circle cx='95' cy='35' r='4'/><circle cx='80' cy='50' r='4'/><circle cx='95' cy='50' r='4'/></g><g fill='#6366f1'><circle cx='150' cy='25' r='4'/><circle cx='165' cy='25' r='4'/><circle cx='180' cy='25' r='4'/><circle cx='150' cy='40' r='4'/><circle cx='165' cy='40' r='4'/><circle cx='180' cy='40' r='4'/><circle cx='150' cy='55' r='4'/><circle cx='165' cy='55' r='4'/><circle cx='180' cy='55' r='4'/></g><text x='215' y='48' font-size='24' fill='#94a3b8'>?</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** The terms are consecutive perfect squares $n^2$.

**Working.** $1=1^2$, $4=2^2$, $9=3^2$, so the next is $4^2=16$.

**Answer.** $16$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: In a non-leap year, $1$ January is a Wednesday. What day is $1$ February?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** January has $31$ days; advance by $31\bmod 7$.

**Working.** $31\bmod 7 = 3$, so Wednesday $+3 =$ Saturday.

**Answer.** Saturday.

questions.subject: Engineering & Environmental Geology
questions.stem: Rock Quality Designation (RQD) is computed from drill core as the percentage of
options: intact pieces ≥10 cm summed over the run length
questions.solution:
**Concept.** RQD = Σ(intact pieces ≥10 cm) / total core run × 100.

**Working.**
Pieces ≥10 cm.

**Answer.** Σ pieces ≥10 cm

questions.subject: Structural Geology
questions.stem: A rock element is under $\sigma_1=150\,$MPa and $\sigma_3=30\,$MPa. The maximum shear stress is _____ MPa.

questions.subject: Structural Geology
questions.stem: At a point $\sigma_x=120$, $\sigma_y=20$, $\tau_{xy}=20\,$MPa. The angle $\theta_p$ of $\sigma_1$ from the x-axis is _____ degrees.

questions.subject: Structural Geology
questions.stem: A planar fault strikes $30^\circ$ and dips $35^\circ$. The plunge of the true-dip line (steepest line on the plane) is _____ degrees.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The true-dip line plunges at the dip angle, in the dip direction.

**Working.**
$$\text{plunge}=\text{dip}=35^\circ.$$

**Answer.** $35^\circ$

**Concept.** The true-dip line plunges at the dip angle, in the dip direction.

**Working.**
$$\text{plunge}=\text{dip}=45^\circ.$$

**Answer.** $45^\circ$
